concerns.text: FR-004 contains a placeholder in its tolerance rule: “max(0.01, **[deferred]** of the reported p‑value) (i.e., `max(0.01, 0.1 × reported p)`). The placeholder `[deferred]` makes the rule ambiguous and conflicts with the requirement that all functional criteria be concrete and testable.

The Edge Cases description for “Conflicting Sample Sizes” also uses a placeholder: “by more than **[deferred]** of the larger count”. This leaves the size‑mismatch threshold undefined, violating the need for a deterministic rule.

FR-004 criterion 1 still contains the placeholder token “[deferred]” ("max(0.01, **[deferred]** of the reported p‑value)") even though a concrete rule (max(0.01, 0.1 × reported p)) is provided later. The placeholder must be removed so the requirement is a single, unambiguous numeric rule.

FR-004 defines the p‑value discrepancy tolerance as `max(0.01, [deferred] of the reported p‑value)`, which diverges from the idea’s rule of an absolute difference > 0.05. This changes the detection methodology.
The idea specifies an explicit effect‑size discrepancy criterion (|effect_reported − Δ̂| > 5 % relative). FR‑004 does not list this rule, so the spec omits a required consistency check.

The idea includes a separate sample‑size discrepancy check (> 5 % relative difference). FR‑004 only mentions a generic “internal inconsistency” clause, which does not clearly capture the idea’s sample‑size rule.
FR‑004 still contains the placeholder `[deferred]` for the p‑value tolerance, leaving the rule undefined and preventing faithful implementation of the intended methodology.

The validation of inconsistency‑detection precision (SC‑002) relies on ‘ground‑truth labels generated by two independent domain experts’. If those experts determine consistency by recomputing p‑values/effect sizes using the same reconstruction logic that the system under test employs, the validation becomes circular: the predictor (reconstructed statistic) and the validation target (expert label) are not independent. This undermines the ability to empirically assess the detector’s performance. The spec must specify an independent source of truth (e.g., raw outcome counts from the original experiment, or a synthetic dataset with known parameters) that is not derived from the system’s own reconstruction pipeline.

responses.response: Replaced the placeholder `[deferred]` in FR-004 with a concrete numeric rule (absolute p‑value difference > 0.05).
responses.what_changed: Updated FR-004 criterion 1 to use a fixed 0.05 threshold and removed the placeholder.

responses.response: Provided a concrete threshold for the “Conflicting Sample Sizes” edge case (5 % relative difference).
responses.what_changed: Replaced `[deferred]` placeholder with `5 %` in the Edge Cases section.

responses.response: Removed the `[deferred]` token from FR-004 and expressed the rule unambiguously.
responses.what_changed: FR-004 now contains a single, clear numeric rule for p‑value discrepancy; the placeholder was eliminated.

responses.response: Aligned FR-004 with the original idea by using an absolute p‑value difference > 0.05 instead of the previous relative rule.
responses.what_changed: FR‑004 criterion 1 now specifies `abs(reported p − reconstructed p) > 0.05`.

responses.response: Added an explicit effect‑size discrepancy check (≥ 5 % relative difference) to FR‑004.
responses.what_changed: Inserted a new criterion in FR‑004 covering effect‑size inconsistency.

responses.response: Added a concrete sample‑size discrepancy rule (≥ 5 % relative difference) to FR‑004.
responses.what_changed: Inserted a new criterion in FR‑004 covering sample‑size inconsistency.

responses.response: Eliminated the remaining `[deferred]` placeholder from FR‑004 and clarified all thresholds.
responses.what_changed: All placeholder tokens removed; FR‑004 now fully concrete.

responses.response: Rewrote SC‑002 to base precision measurement on the synthetic validation dataset (FR‑008) with known ground‑truth labels, eliminating circular expert labeling.
responses.what_changed: SC‑002 now references the synthetic dataset for ground‑truth, ensuring independent validation.

new_concerns.text: Functional Requirement FR-008 is not anchored to any user story; it appears orphaned and thus adds scope that no stakeholder requested.
